title: The Drowned Alphabet

features.name: stone-letters
features.desc-on-touch: The alphabet drowned here and left its shape in the stones. You cannot read the letters but you recognize the grammar of water.

features.name: floating-language
features.desc-on-touch: Words drift here, neither sinking nor rising. Language has learned to breathe water and has forgotten how to drown.

features.name: bed-current
features.desc-on-touch: The current here reads—it is the river's own voice, learning syntax from the stones the water has polished.

features.name: dissolution-point
features.desc-on-touch: Here the alphabet ceases to be individual letters and becomes the roar of water itself—meaning dissolving into sound.

features.name: relearning
features.desc-on-touch: The water is patient. It is teaching itself the alphabet again—slowly—using only the sound of stone.

inscription: where water learned language and forgot it again, teaching itself by the sound of stone

file.name: Alphabetic_Water_Behaviour_Recovery_Study_Incomplete.txt
file.note: The alphabet drowns. The water relearns. Syntax emerges from erosion. Archive is taking notes.

exits.label: back to the language of stones where water still holds words
deeper down the bed where letters have become pure current
toward the estuary where the alphabet joins the sea
